name: Ria Money Transfer & Currency Exchange
description_short: Ria Money Transfer operates a global money transfer network with physical agent locations offering cash send/receive, bill payments, and currency exchange services across 40+ countries.
description_long:
Ria Money Transfer is an international money transfer company that operates through a distributed network of physical agent locations rather than as a traditional bank or standalone storefront. The company has established partnerships with retail locations including 7-Eleven stores, check cashing facilities, grocery stores, pharmacies, and various multiservice businesses across the United States and internationally.

Ria's service offerings span multiple money transfer methods: customers can send money online, through a mobile app, via WhatsApp, or in person at agent locations. Recipients can access funds through bank deposits, cash pickups, digital wallets, home delivery, or ATM withdrawals. The company also provides bill payment services, currency conversion tools, IBAN calculators, and money transfer tracking. Their agent network includes dedicated check cashing partners like PLS Check Cashers and independent multiservice businesses, making services accessible in diverse neighborhoods and communities.

What distinguishes Ria is its agent-based model rather than direct branch presence. The company leverages existing retail infrastructure—particularly convenience stores and check cashing outlets—to reach customers, which keeps operational costs lower than traditional remittance companies. This approach allows 24-hour service availability through 7-Eleven partners while maintaining presence in immigrant-serving neighborhoods through independent agents. The platform also emphasizes digital-first options with WhatsApp and mobile app capabilities alongside in-person transactions.

A realistic assessment: Ria functions primarily as a money transfer network operator, not as a financial institution holding consumer accounts. Customers using Ria should verify exact fees, exchange rates, and delivery timeframes for their specific corridor, as these vary by agent location and destination country. The agent-based model means service quality and availability depend on individual partner locations, which may have varying hours and capabilities.
address: El Super, 3190 E Tulare St, Fresno, CA 93702

company_info.headquarters: El Super, 3190 E Tulare St, Fresno, CA 93702

pros: Global reach to 40+ countries across Africa, Asia, Europe, Latin America, North America, and Oceania
Multiple send methods including online, mobile app, WhatsApp, and in-person at agent locations
Flexible receive options: bank deposit, cash pickup, digital wallet, home delivery, and ATM withdrawal
Extended hours through 7-Eleven partnerships offering 24-hour service at select locations
Multiple agent locations accessible in diverse communities including grocery stores and pharmacies
Free currency converter and IBAN calculator tools on website
Bill payment services available at many agent locations
cons: Service quality and hours depend on individual agent locations, creating inconsistent customer experience
No direct branches—reliance on third-party agents means limited control over service standards
Website does not display exchange rates or transfer fees, requiring customers to visit locations or contact agents
Limited information about regulatory oversight and consumer protections on public-facing website
Agent network includes various business types with potentially different compliance standards
best_for: Immigrants and expatriates sending money to family in specific countries with established Ria corridors
Customers seeking convenient in-person money transfer at retail locations near their home or work
Users who prefer multiple payment methods and fund delivery options for international transfers
diagnosis: Ria Money Transfer is best for individuals needing to send money internationally to 40+ countries through convenient agent locations, particularly those in immigrant communities with established partnerships. Primary caveat: this is a money transfer network operator, not a bank, so customers should verify specific fees, exchange rates, and delivery timeframes with individual agents before transferring funds.
services: In-person money transfers at agent locations
meta_description: Ria Money Transfer offers cash send/receive, bill payments, and currency exchange across 40+ countries.
